# Code Of Conduct

## Purpose

This project is maintained as a safety-conscious repair and research effort. Contributors are expected to collaborate respectfully, share information responsibly, and avoid pressuring others into risky hardware experiments.

## Expected Behavior

- Be respectful, patient, and constructive in issues, pull requests, reviews, and discussions.
- Focus feedback on the work, the evidence, and the safety implications.
- Assume good intent while still challenging technical claims that are weak, unsafe, or unsupported.
- Share enough context for others to reproduce your reasoning without oversharing sensitive material.
- Respect maintainer decisions about safety, scope, and publication boundaries.

## Unacceptable Behavior

- Harassment, personal attacks, intimidation, or discriminatory behavior.
- Dismissing safety concerns or encouraging others to ignore test precautions.
- Posting secrets, private identifiers, raw sensitive captures, or unreviewed vendor-derived material.
- Misrepresenting test results, hiding known risks, or pressuring others to run destructive experiments.
- Repeatedly derailing discussions after a maintainer has made a documented decision.

## Scope

This Code of Conduct applies to repository spaces and project collaboration, including issues, pull requests, code review, documentation discussions, shared logs or traces, and any other project communication channels controlled by the maintainer.

## Reporting

Report conduct concerns to the project owner, Joseph, through a private channel when possible. Include links, screenshots, or other relevant context so the report can be reviewed quickly.

If the concern is primarily about unsafe test instructions, sensitive data exposure, or publication of restricted materials, also reference the relevant safety or distribution-policy document in your report.

## Enforcement

The maintainer may remove content, restrict participation, or close discussions that violate this Code of Conduct or create an unsafe collaboration environment.

Responses should be proportional to the behavior and the risk created for the community or the hardware under test.

## Relationship To Other Policies

This document governs contributor behavior. Technical safety rules, reverse-engineering boundaries, and publication constraints are defined separately in the project's safety and policy documents.
